title: HexClad Paring Knife, 9 cm Japanese Damascus Stainless Steel Blade, Full Tang Construction, Pakkawood Handle
features: 66 layers of Damascus steel for a harder blade that stays sharp longer with an ergonomic Pakka wood handle for precision and control. Made using the Honbazuke 3-step method, an advanced heat treatment that combines hardness and flexibility to create an exceptional 12 degree sharpness.
This 9cm paring knife comes out of the sharp box and ready to use, whether peeling a potato, deveining shrimp or preparing a dish of crudité, thanks to its Japanese Damascus steel construction.

Our knives are expertly crafted tools that maintain their sharpness for extended periods with proper care. Do not wash them in the dishwasher. Hand wash only. It is recommended to sharpen them frequently.
This 3.5" paring knife has a total length of 7.5" and a 3" Japanese Damascus stainless steel blade. Its 11cm Pakka wood handle offers a comfortable, ergonomic grip.

HexClad flatware has a lifetime warranty against manufacturing defects. Does not cover damage from improper use or storage, accident, loss or theft. Cutting on hard surfaces, use in dishwashers, dull, rusty, faded or bent/broken blades and use in food service will also void this warranty.
description: Product Description Previous page Next page 1 Discover HexClad Knives 2 60 Rockwell Rating 3 67 Layers of Steel 4 Balanced Full-Tang Handle 5 Pakkawood Handle How does your Return Policy work? All official purchases have 30 calendar days from the date of purchase to return an item. All returns require a receipt or proof of purchase. Please note that the customer is responsible for paying any return shipments. Are HexClad Japanese Damascus Knives dishwasher safe? Like any high-quality knife, we highly recommend hand washing your HexClad knives. Use a gentle dish soap, rinse and towel dry immediately to remove any lingering moisture from your blade. Is the knife blade made from stainless steel? Yes, HexClad Japanese Damascus Steel Knives are made of AUS-10 stainless steel. Will HexClad Japanese Damascus Steel Knives stick to any magnetic knife block? Yes, they will work on magnetic knife blocks like the HexClad Magnetic Walnut Knife Block Holder. Will I have to sharpen my HexClad Japanese Damascus Steel Knives? Though they are designed to stay sharp for a long time, you will have to occasionally sharpen these knives depending on the level of use.
